headlines: Morgan Stanley’s spot Bitcoin ETF (MSBT) nears launch
Stablecoin supply hits record $315B in Q1 as USDC gains
U.S. charges suspect in $54M Uranium Finance DeFi exploit
Japanese firm Metaplanet buys 5,075 BTC, becomes 3rd‑largest public treasury

JPMorgan: crypto fund flows fell to $11B in Q1 — big slowdown vs. last year (2025) / Q1 2026 drop noted (analysis) (clarify date) — source: The Block (Apr 3) — summarised below)  NOTE: The Block article reports Q1 2026 flows fell to $11B (Apr 3, 2026) — user asked for last 24 hours—this was within that window (Apr 3). Please verify directly if you need transaction‑level data) — placeholder for more accurate timestamping if needed]  Please confirm you want direct links or a different selection of stories.

articles.title: Morgan Stanley’s MSBT amendment signals near‑term spot Bitcoin ETF launch
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley filed an updated S‑1 (Amendment No. 4) for the Morgan Stanley Bitcoin Trust, naming NYSE Arca as the planned listing exchange and showing operational details (seed capital, custodians and a low 0.14% fee). Markets see the filing as a strong sign the bank’s spot BTC ETF (ticker MSBT) could start trading soon — a major development for institutional Bitcoin access.

articles.title: Stablecoin supply tops $315 billion in Q1 as USDC rises and USDT slips
articles.summary: Stablecoin total supply reached a record about $315B in Q1 2026. USDC gained market share while USDT declined, and stablecoins continued to dominate trading volume as investors sought a safe, liquid on‑ramp during a cautious quarter.

articles.title: U.S. indicts suspect tied to $54M Uranium Finance DeFi hack
articles.summary: U.S. prosecutors charged a Maryland man in connection with two 2021 exploits of Uranium Finance that drained roughly $53–54 million. The case underscores rising enforcement focus on historic DeFi exploits and could shape future criminal enforcement in decentralized finance.

articles.title: Metaplanet adds 5,075 BTC in Q1 and becomes the 3rd‑largest corporate Bitcoin treasury
articles.summary: Tokyo‑listed Metaplanet disclosed Q1 purchases of about 5,075 BTC (≈$405M), bringing its total to ~40,177 BTC and making it the third‑largest public company holder. Large corporate treasuries remain a meaningful demand source for Bitcoin.

articles.title: JPMorgan: crypto fund flows slow — $11B in Q1 2026
articles.summary: JPMorgan research shows crypto flows fell to roughly $11 billion in Q1 2026 — about one‑third of the inflows seen in the same quarter last year — signaling a marked slowdown in investor demand and heavier reliance on corporate/treasury purchases and specialized products.

articles.title: Dubai’s VARA issues formal rules for crypto derivatives — retail allowed with limits
articles.summary: Dubai’s Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority released a framework for exchange‑traded crypto derivatives that permits retail access under suitability checks, margin controls and leverage caps — part of wider efforts to build a regulated derivatives market for digital assets.

articles.title: Bitcoin drifts below $67K amid geopolitical and political headlines
articles.summary: Bitcoin slipped to the mid‑$60K range on April 3 as markets reacted to geopolitical tensions and U.S. political developments. Traders flagged thinner holiday liquidity and risk‑off flows into stablecoins and cash, pushing short‑term price pressure on BTC and other large caps.

articles.title: Google research highlights quantum computing threat to current crypto keys
articles.summary: Google published research suggesting certain quantum advances could reduce the resources needed to attack elliptic‑curve cryptography used by Bitcoin and Ethereum. The paper warns long‑term cryptographic risks and adds urgency to post‑quantum planning for wallets, exchanges and infrastructure.
